describes: The executable conformance test-case catalog referenced by spec §10.3. Machine-readable twin of SUITE.md (same case ids). A runner in any language drives these cases against vectors/vectors.json (byte-exact KAT inputs) plus the inline self-contained constructions here.

outcomes.match: known-answer test: recompute the operation over the fixed input; result MUST equal the committed expected value
outcomes.accept: validation predicate: a conforming node MUST accept the input
outcomes.reject: validation predicate: a conforming node MUST reject; error_code names the §21 registry code and its Action disposition applies

status_values.vectored: byte-backed by a named entry in vectors/vectors.json
status_values.construction-todo: byte-exact construction recipe given; vector still to be generated as the subsystem gains a fixed-input KAT
status_values.manual-attestation: a client-UX, in-product-disclosure or deployment/process MUST with no wire bytes to recompute (§22.7's explicit-publish-act and irrevocability disclosures; §4.4.10a's Bootstrap degradation disclosure and no-anonymity-claim rule; §7.11.4/§9.11's gateway posture; §7.1b's process/privilege separation); verified by implementer/deployment review, not by a byte-level runner

vectors_count_note: vectors_count is the number of entries in vectors/vectors.json; referenced_vectors_count (33) is how many of them the byte-backed cases currently drive (several drive more than one case). The remaining 35 are pre-generated for construction-todo families not yet wired to a case.
notes: reject cases map to spec §21 (141-code registry); error_code + error_name + disposition (Action column) are normative.
self-contained CBOR reject cases assert a deterministic-CBOR (RFC 8949 §4.2 / §18.1.1) decoder rejects the given bytes.
construction-todo cases are non-deterministic to seal (sealed Envelope/Payload) or belong to subsystems not yet vectored (README 'Coverage vs. deferred').

cases.checks: decode(encode(Identity)) is byte-identical; integer-keyed map, ascending keys
DeviceCert deterministic re-encode is byte-identical
Payload deterministic re-encode is byte-identical
Envelope deterministic re-encode is byte-identical
reject non-shortest (non-preferred) integer encoding

input.derivation: uint 23 encoded in two bytes (major 0, ai 24, 0x17); preferred form is single byte 0x17

cases.checks: reject indefinite-length item

input.derivation: indefinite-length array (major 4, ai 31) closed by break 0xff

cases.checks: reject map whose keys are not bytewise-ascending

input.derivation: map {2:0, 1:0}; keys 2 then 1 are descending, violating deterministic key order

cases.checks: reject map with a duplicate key
reject any floating-point value

input.derivation: half-precision float 1.5 (major 7, ai 25); no floats appear in DMTAP wire objects

cases.checks: reject CBOR undefined (and NaN/Infinity/undefined-tag)

input.derivation: simple(23) = undefined (major 7, ai 23); MUST NOT appear

cases.checks: reject a wire map carrying an optional key whose value is null (absent optional MUST be omitted, never null)

input.construction: take vector cbor_envelope, insert key 5 (epoch) => 0xf6 (null) in sorted position, re-encode

cases.checks: a decoder of a signed object rejects any unknown integer key >= 64 (fail closed; keeps the signing preimage unambiguous)

input.construction: take vector cbor_payload, insert key 64 (0x1840) => 0 in sorted position, re-encode

cases.checks: id = 0x1e || BLAKE3-256(bytes), empty input
content address of a 5-byte input
content address of a human phrase
content address of a 4096-byte input
Envelope.id recomputes from ciphertext, before decryption
tampered ciphertext fails the address check; drop before decrypt
deterministic Ed25519 (RFC 8032 Test 1 cross-check)
deterministic Ed25519 (RFC 8032 Test 2 cross-check)
Ed25519 over the domain-separated preimage DS || msg
a valid signature verifies
one-bit message tamper fails verification (fail closed)
one-bit signature tamper fails verification (fail closed)
sender_sig over DS || id || det_cbor(to) || u64be(ts) || u8(kind) || challenge_enc (absent => 0xf6), under ephemeral sender_key
sender_sig verifies under sender_key before any decryption
Payload.sig over DS || BLAKE3-256(det_cbor(Payload minus sig)) under the IK
deterministic 8-word key-name (+ checksum verifies), all-zero key
key-name of all-0x01 key
key-name of all-0x02 key
key-name of a real Ed25519 public key
distinct keys yield distinct names (keyname_key_ones != keyname_key_twos)

expect.note: the two names MUST differ

cases.checks: a single mistyped word fails the folded checksum (fail closed)

expect.note: checksum_verifies == false

cases.checks: deterministic pair fingerprint / safety number of two identity keys
safety number is order-independent (swapping keys yields the identical value)

expect.note: MUST equal DMTAP-SAFE-01

cases.checks: unknown suite 0x00 rejected on decode (never guess)
suite id 0x03 (reserved AEAD-diverse) is a known reserved id and decodes; an object whose crypto USES 0x03 MUST still fail closed until the suite is implemented
unknown suite 0x05 rejected
unknown suite 0xff rejected
known suite id 0x01 (classical) decodes
suite id 0x02 (reserved PQ) is a known id and decodes; an object whose crypto USES 0x02 MUST still fail closed until PQ is implemented
unregistered suite 0x04 rejected on decode (never guess)
unknown v/suite rejected first, before any crypto
cases.construction: Envelope with v=1 (or an unknown suite)

cases.checks: id mismatch dropped before decryption
sender_sig failure dropped (cheap, pre-decryption)
cases.construction: mote_sender_sig fixture with one signature bit flipped

cases.checks: to that does not resolve to this node/group is dropped
cases.construction: Envelope.to = KeyTag(a key this node does not hold)

cases.checks: cold sender forged/invalid challenge discarded silently, not acked
cases.construction: cold-sender Envelope with a tampered ChallengeResponse

cases.checks: cold sender absent/under-threshold proof deferred to requests area, rate-limited, NOT acked (deferred = no ack)
cases.construction: cold-sender Envelope, challenge absent

cases.checks: ciphertext that fails to decrypt is dropped
cases.construction: Envelope with corrupt ciphertext (id recomputed to keep step 2 valid)

cases.checks: Payload.sig failure => discard silently and do NOT ack (fail closed)
cases.construction: sealed Payload with tampered sig

cases.checks: decrypted from mismatching the pinned identity => warning; MUST NOT silently repin
cases.construction: known-contact Envelope whose Payload.from != pinned identity

cases.checks: suite-ratchet: Envelope.suite below the contact's pinned high-water-mark is a downgrade => requests area + warning; MUST NOT accept, MUST NOT ratchet HWM down
cases.construction: pinned contact HWM=0x02, inbound Envelope.suite=0x01

cases.checks: a duplicate id already held is acked immediately without re-processing
cases.construction: re-deliver an already-stored id

cases.checks: ack owed ONLY for inbox delivery; a deferred cold MOTE is durably held (requests retention 30d, §16.5) but NO ack is sent
cases.construction: cold MOTE deferred at step 6

expect.note: held in requests area, no receipt emitted

cases.checks: a kind the node cannot validate is ignored and MUST NOT be acked
cases.construction: Envelope.kind = 0x40 (reserved, unimplemented)

cases.checks: ts outside +/-120s skew dropped for cold senders (MAY be lenient for known contacts)
cases.construction: Envelope.ts = now + 10 min

cases.checks: an expired MOTE (Payload.expires past) is dropped (cooperative hint, not a security guarantee)
cases.construction: Payload.expires in the past

cases.checks: an Identity whose sig (any suite entry) fails is rejected
cases.construction: cbor_identity with a tampered sig entry

cases.checks: an Identity.version <= the pinned version is rejected (anti-rollback)
cases.construction: pin version=n, then present a validly-signed version=n-1

cases.checks: a broken prev hash chain is rejected
cases.construction: Identity.prev != hash of the pinned prior Identity

cases.checks: KT fail-closed: log unreachable at first-contact pinning => MUST NOT silently TOFU-pin; block or hard-warn
cases.construction: first contact with the KT endpoint unreachable

cases.checks: a DeviceCert with invalid sig, or caps exceeding IK authorization, is rejected
cases.construction: cbor_device_cert with a tampered sig

cases.checks: empty suite intersection => delivery fails closed, no silent downgrade
cases.construction: sender suites intersect recipient Identity.suites = empty

cases.checks: Sphinx packet constant-length on the bucket ladder {16, 64} KiB; wrong length rejected (neither the 2 KiB nor the 8 KiB rung can hold a conformant PQ envelope: under suite 0x02 a minimal MOTE is 11 967 B before any body, §4.4.1)
cases.construction: Sphinx packet off the {16, 64} KiB bucket ladder

cases.checks: a cached MixDirectory is verified against the client's pinned KT log quorum (> n/2), never under a directory authority — there is none (§4.4.2); an absent authority signature is not a failure, an unverifiable descriptor is (the per-descriptor MUST NOT is DMTAP-FLEET-02)
cases.construction: cached MixDirectory whose contents fail independent verification against the client's pinned log quorum

cases.checks: per-epoch replay cache drops a replayed mix packet
cases.construction: replay a previously-seen packet within the epoch

cases.checks: fail-closed no-downgrade: a forced private->fast drop is refused, never silent
cases.construction: attempt to downgrade a private-tier flow to fast

cases.checks: loop-cover / active-attack suspicion is surfaced, not ignored
cases.construction: trigger loop-cover attack detection

cases.checks: a stale MixNodeDescriptor is not used
cases.construction: MixNodeDescriptor past its freshness window

cases.checks: high-security profile and PQ-Sphinx are OPTIONAL (negotiated, not required at Private)
cases.construction: peer without high-security profile / PQ-Sphinx

expect.note: optional capabilities

cases.checks: GroupEvent/GroupState committer_sig verifies under the committer's IK-authorized device key
cases.construction: GroupEvent with a tampered committer_sig

expect.note: committer-sig / member-sig verification failure

cases.checks: two Commits at the same log position with the same predecessor => committer fork
cases.construction: two conflicting Commits at one position

cases.checks: wrong MLS epoch key selection is rejected
cases.construction: Envelope.epoch referencing a mismatched epoch

cases.checks: Manifest.id = RFC 6962 Merkle root over ordered chunk hashes with domain-separated leaf(0x00)/node(0x01) prefixes
cases.construction: compute MTH over a fixed ordered chunk-hash list

cases.checks: a fetched chunk whose hash != its Manifest.chunks entry is rejected
cases.construction: chunk with a flipped byte

cases.checks: a file routed on the wrong size-tier path is rejected
cases.construction: large file offered on the inline/normal path

cases.checks: a Manifest MUST NOT carry the file key (key rides the sealed MOTE, not the manifest)
cases.construction: Manifest carrying an embedded file key

cases.checks: the content address is over ciphertext: the same plaintext under two different per-file keys yields two different Manifest.id values (no cross-user/plaintext dedup; CAS-confirmation defense)
cases.construction: encrypt one fixed plaintext under two distinct per-file random keys; compute both manifest roots; assert they differ

cases.checks: a Referenced (> 25 MiB) ManifestRef missing durability, or with an unknown class / cluster-replicated replicas<1 / pinned without retention, is rejected fail-closed
cases.construction: a Referenced ManifestRef with no Durability (and variants: class=99 unknown; class=2 replicas=0; class=3 no retention)

cases.checks: a pushed Inline/Attached file exceeding the recipient's inbound spool cap for that sender is refused fail-closed (spool-fill storage DoS), never silently accepted or dropped
cases.construction: an Attached push whose size exceeds the per-sender inbound spool cap

cases.checks: a pinned(term) (class=3) fetch past its elapsed retention is rejected (the host MAY have GC'd)
cases.construction: a Durability class=3 with retention in the past, fetched after expiry

cases.checks: a Referenced origin-hold file with no reachable holder and no satisfiable durability contract fails at the file level, distinct from a single missing chunk (0x0803)
cases.construction: a Referenced origin-hold ManifestRef whose origin and all swarm holders are unreachable

cases.checks: a gateway attestation that fails to verify under a trusted key is rejected
cases.construction: gateway attestation with an untrusted/invalid signature

cases.checks: invalid DKIM delegation is rejected
cases.construction: DKIM delegation with an invalid signature

cases.checks: an outbound DMTAP->legacy relay from a sender the gateway has neither authenticated (no GatewayAuthz / key-registered relationship) nor been paid by (no valid redeemable postage) is refused fail-closed; a valid mesh sender_sig alone does NOT authorize egress (open-relay prevention)
cases.construction: present a signed mail MOTE for a legacy address to a gateway with no authorization relationship for the sender key and no attached postage

cases.checks: a MOTE renders to/from the native JMAP object model (the node's only client surface) without loss of the §8-required fields
cases.construction: MOTE <-> JMAP mapping of a fixed mail MOTE

expect.note: round-trip preserves required fields

cases.checks: Assertion.sig over DS || BLAKE3-256(det_cbor([rp_origin,nonce,issued_at,exp,aud,scope,cnf])) under the IK-authorized device key; scope is [] when the Challenge omits it and is inside the signed preimage (scope-binding, §18.7.2)
cases.construction: sign a fixed 7-field auth array (scope defaults to [])

cases.checks: an assertion whose rp_origin/aud mismatch the issued Challenge is rejected
cases.construction: Assertion with a mismatched rp_origin

cases.checks: a replayed nonce is rejected
cases.construction: replay a previously-seen nonce

cases.checks: an expired Challenge is rejected
cases.construction: Assertion answering an expired Challenge

cases.checks: the session is bound ONLY to cnf (not the signing key) and MUST reject on cnf mismatch
cases.construction: bind a session and re-present with a mismatched cnf

expect.note: bound to cnf; mismatch => reject

cases.checks: a DeniablePayload carrying any signature field is rejected (a signature would defeat repudiation)
cases.construction: DeniablePayload with a signature field present

cases.checks: a deniable session to a peer that has not advertised deniable-1:1 is refused; the client surfaces the choice, never silently downgrades
cases.construction: initiate a deniable session to a peer without the deniable-1:1 capability token

cases.checks: a DeniableMessage whose Double-Ratchet AEAD tag (shared-key MAC) fails is dropped; substitutes for Payload.sig (§20.2 fork)
cases.construction: DeniableMessage with a flipped AEAD tag byte

cases.checks: an invalid/exhausted DeniablePrekeyBundle (sig/spk_sig/idk_sig fail, or no unspent prekey) is rejected
cases.construction: DeniablePrekeyBundle with a tampered spk_sig, or exhausted of unspent prekeys

cases.checks: X3DH/PQXDH over the dedicated idk (NOT XEdDSA-from-IK): a DeniableInit whose idk_a_cert does not certify idk_a under ik_a, or whose key agreement fails, is rejected; last-resort-only first-message replay is caught
cases.construction: DeniableInit whose idk_a_cert does not certify idk_a under ik_a (or a failing key agreement)

cases.checks: an org-managed (escrowed-key) account presented without its org-managed custody marker is rejected — undisclosed escrow
cases.construction: present an escrowed-key account with the org-managed custody marker stripped

cases.checks: a DirEntry whose name → ik does not forward-verify against DNS+KT is rendered unverified, never used to address mail
cases.construction: build a DirEntry whose name→ik disagrees with the DNS+KT-resolved ik

cases.checks: a DomainDirectory not signed by the pinned domain authority is rejected
cases.construction: sign a DomainDirectory with a key other than the pinned domain authority

cases.checks: a CapabilityToken whose link grants more than its parent (attenuation broken), is expired, or is invoked beyond its rights is rejected
cases.construction: forge a child CapabilityToken granting rights not held by its parent

cases.checks: a validly-formed CapabilityToken covered by a published CapabilityRevocation (from its issuer/ancestor) is denied
cases.construction: present a well-formed token matched by a published CapabilityRevocation from its ancestor

cases.checks: two validly-signed STHs of one log with equal tree_size but differing root_hash (or no consistency proof) => equivocation: HALT + alert + publish evidence
cases.construction: two validly-signed STHs of one log_id with equal tree_size and differing root_hash

expect.note: companion conclusion code ERR_KT_EQUIVOCATION (0x0107); publish the conflicting STHs as transferable evidence (§3.5.2(d))

cases.checks: a name -> ik binding not attested by a > n/2 quorum of the pinned log set fails closed -> OOB
cases.construction: a name -> ik binding attested by <= n/2 of the pinned log set

cases.checks: a SignedTreeHead older than the freshness window (freeze attack) is treated as stale and refreshed
cases.construction: a self-consistent SignedTreeHead older than the STH freshness window

cases.checks: an InclusionProof whose committed leaf != the recomputed Identity-entry leaf-hash 0x1e||BLAKE3-256(0x00||det_cbor([name,ik,version,identity_id])) is rejected
cases.construction: an InclusionProof whose committed leaf_hash differs from the recomputed Identity-entry leaf-hash

cases.checks: an attestation-gated context rejects a device whose key_protection/attestation is absent or fails against the platform root (advisory — never overrides §1.4 authority)
cases.construction: enter an attestation-gated context with attestation evidence absent or failing the platform root

cases.checks: evidence older than the re-attestation cadence (≤ 90 d), past its window, or chaining only to a retired root is treated as expired → re-attest
cases.construction: present attestation evidence older than the 90-day cadence or chaining only to a retired root

cases.checks: a Profile whose sig (DS-tag DMTAP-v0/profile) does not verify under the identity's IK / an IK-authorized device key is rejected; the prior pinned profile (or fallback ladder) is used
cases.construction: a Profile with a tampered sig

cases.checks: a Profile whose avatar.hash is present but the bytes fetched from avatar.url do not content-address (0x1e||BLAKE3-256) to it MUST NOT be displayed; the client falls back down the §3.9.5 ladder (key-derived identicon -> initials) and warns
cases.construction: a Profile with avatar.hash present and tampered fetched avatar bytes

cases.checks: a WakePing carrying any field beyond the opaque sealed token (key 1), or whose opened plaintext bears sender/subject/recipient/content, MUST be rejected: a wake is content-free and sender-blind (optional push-wake capability)
cases.construction: a WakePing with an extra map key (or a decoded plaintext bearing sender/content) alongside key 1

cases.checks: a PushSubscription whose sig does not verify under an IK-authorized device_key (§1.2) MUST be rejected and never woken against: the subscription must be authenticated to the identity (optional push-wake capability)
cases.construction: a PushSubscription with a tampered sig / a device_key not authorized by a current DeviceCert

cases.checks: a ClusterSyncFrame/ClusterOp from a device whose DeviceCert is absent/invalid or revoked (KeyRotation-excluded) under the owner's IK is refused — replication is mutually authenticated
cases.construction: present a ClusterSyncFrame signed by a device that is not a non-revoked member of the identity's cluster

cases.checks: a recon summary whose RangeFingerprint.fp does not recompute over the receiver's ids in [lo,hi) (forged Merkle fingerprint) is rejected and reconciliation re-driven
cases.construction: present a recon ClusterSyncFrame with a RangeFingerprint.fp that does not equal the hash of the receiver's sorted ids in the range

cases.checks: a journal-replay segment whose prev hash-chain does not verify (a fork/rewrite of the owner's own log) is halted on, analogous to a committer fork
cases.construction: present a journal ClusterSyncFrame whose JournalEntry.prev does not equal the hash of the prior entry

cases.checks: a ClusterOp with an unknown kind, an OR-Set remove citing an unknown add-tag, an HLC wall beyond the skew bound, or embedding a DeniablePayload/its plaintext (§5.2.1) is rejected
cases.construction: present a ClusterOp with an HLC wall > skew bound ahead of the receiver (or embedding a DeniablePayload)

cases.checks: convergence (strong eventual consistency): two replicas applying concurrent OR-Set add/remove + per-field LWW ops in any order reach the identical state — add-wins-over-unseen-remove; greater HLC (wall,counter,device) wins each field deterministically
cases.construction: apply the same set of concurrent OR-Set and LWW ClusterOps in two different orders on two replicas; assert identical resulting state

cases.checks: a name in the identity's own Identity.names whose forward name->ik binding (DNS+KT) resolves to a different key is rendered unverified and MUST NOT be displayed as authenticated nor used to address mail
cases.construction: list a victim's address in Identity.names whose forward DNS+KT binding resolves to a different key

cases.checks: a revoked alias (dropped in a newer signed Identity, its binding retired) used off a stale cache to address the identity is refused; the key and the identity's other aliases are unaffected
cases.construction: address the identity via an alias removed in the current signed Identity version / retired in DNS+KT

cases.checks: multiple verified aliases (distinct name, same ik/identity_id) resolve to the same identity — recognized as one person/one key, pinned per-key
cases.construction: resolve three verified aliases (random/vanity/byod) that share one ik/identity_id; assert all yield the same identity

cases.checks: a name-chain resolution whose two binding directions disagree — an on-chain name->ik record naming a key that does not claim the name in its signed Identity.names, or a claimed name whose chain record resolves to a different key — is rendered unverified and MUST NOT be used to address mail
cases.construction: resolve a name-chain (.eth/.sol) name where the on-chain name->ik record and the key's self-asserted Identity.names disagree; assert the binding is rejected as unverified

cases.checks: a name in a resolver type the verifier does not implement, or that is unregistered, is treated as unresolvable and fails closed — the unknown-implies-reject-never-guess discipline; the identity stays reachable via its other resolvers and the key-name (§3.9.6)
cases.construction: present a name tagged with a resolver-type the node does not implement/registered; assert it is treated as unresolvable and no binding is guessed

cases.checks: two independent resolvers returning different ik for the same name is surfaced as a potential attack, never silently reconciled: the client MUST NOT pin, MUST alert, and MUST fall back to KT-quorum or OOB verification
cases.construction: query two resolvers (e.g. dns _dmtap pointer and a name-chain record) for one name that return different ik; assert no pin, a security alert, and fallback to KT-quorum/OOB

cases.checks: an encoded gateway alias localpart.nativedomain@gateway.domain that does not reversibly decode to exactly one (localpart, nativedomain) (ambiguous escaping) or exceeds RFC 5321 limits is rejected — the gateway MUST NOT guess a native address
cases.construction: present an encoded alias local-part with ambiguous/illegal escaping or exceeding 64 octets

cases.checks: inbound legacy mail to a random-mode alias with no live GatewayAliasMap row (missing/expired/burned) is answered as 'no such user', not silently dropped
cases.construction: deliver inbound legacy mail to a random gateway alias whose GatewayAliasMap row is absent/expired/burned

cases.checks: the encoded local-part round-trips: encode(localpart, nativedomain) (escape '-'->'--', '.'->'-.', join with a top-level '.') then decode yields the original (localpart, nativedomain) — deterministic KAT, e.g. imran+mydomain.com -> imran.mydomain-.com -> back
cases.construction: encode ('imran','mydomain.com') then decode; assert the pair round-trips exactly

cases.checks: user-visible degradation: a client operating on the Bootstrap profile MUST show that metadata privacy is degraded and MUST say why — how many attested mixes and how many disjoint ASNs the network currently offers against the Standard bar (§16.3). Silence converts a temporary shortfall into an invisible one
cases.construction: client-UX review with the node in a fleet below Standard's bar; no wire bytes to recompute

expect.outcome: non-conformant if the degradation is not shown, or is shown without the current-vs-required mix/ASN counts

cases.checks: no anonymity claim in-product: while Bootstrap is in force an implementation or operator MUST NOT present the private tier as anonymous, MUST NOT report an anonymity-set size, and MUST NOT describe the traffic as unlinkable — §4.4.11's prohibition as a property of the profile itself
cases.construction: review of in-product copy, status surfaces and operator-facing material while Bootstrap is in force

expect.outcome: non-conformant if any of the three claims appears while Bootstrap is in force

cases.checks: auto-upgrade is mandatory: a node MUST re-evaluate its derived fleet view each mix-key epoch and MUST move to Standard as soon as Standard's bar is satisfiable (>=3 hops one per stratified layer, >=3 disjoint attested operators, >=3 disjoint ASNs, guard sample per §16.3). Remaining on Bootstrap with a satisfiable Standard bar is a silent permanent downgrade
cases.construction: derived fleet view below Standard's bar at epoch e (Bootstrap in force); grow the view to satisfy Standard at e+1; step the node's per-epoch re-evaluation with no user action

expect.note: in-force profile = Standard from epoch e+1 without user action; staying on Bootstrap is non-conformant

cases.checks: never fall back: once a node has operated at Standard it MUST NOT return to Bootstrap. If the fleet later shrinks below Standard's bar the sender holds and retries (FAIL-QUEUED) and surfaces the refusal past the retry deadline; it MUST NOT rebuild the path at Bootstrap's bar. Without this rule an attacker who kills mixes forces every sender onto the degraded profile — the §4.4.9 downgrade attack under a friendlier name
cases.construction: node already operating at Standard; shrink the derived fleet below Standard's bar (kill attested mixes / collapse ASN diversity); attempt a private-tier send

expect.action: FAIL-QUEUED (§10.7.0): hold in the retry queue and retry; MUST NOT carry the MOTE at Bootstrap's bar and MUST NOT refuse the enqueue

cases.checks: per-contact ratchet: profile level ratchets per correspondent exactly like the §1.3 suite high-water-mark — once any message in a relationship has been carried at Standard (or High-security), a Bootstrap-tier send to that contact MUST fail closed. The granularity is deliberate: a new contact on a still-small network MUST remain reachable at Bootstrap in the same fleet state, so an attacker must defeat one relationship at a time
cases.construction: contact A with a Standard-carried message in its history and contact B never carried above Bootstrap; with the fleet below Standard's bar, attempt a private-tier send to each

expect.note: reject for contact A (Standard in its history); accept for contact B (never carried above Bootstrap) — a global rather than per-contact mark fails one branch or the other

cases.checks: the fleet view is reconstructable from KT logs alone: with no served MixDirectory available a client MUST still compute its epoch fleet view from its pinned log quorum — descriptors that (i) self-verify under node_ik, (ii) carry a valid _dmtap-mix operator attestation, (iii) name a current-epoch Sphinx key, and (iv) appear with a valid inclusion proof in > n/2 of the pinned logs. An implementation that cannot build a private path without a served directory has re-created the authority §4.4.2 deleted
cases.construction: populate the pinned log set with n descriptors, some failing exactly one of the four predicates; withhold every MixDirectory cache; ask the client for its epoch fleet view

expect.note: derived view = exactly the descriptors satisfying all four predicates, and identical to the view derived when a valid cache is present

cases.checks: a cache is never an authority: a client MUST NOT accept a served/cached MixDirectory containing a descriptor it cannot independently verify against its own log quorum (any of the four derivation predicates failing), and MUST reject a cache whose version is older-or-equal to one already accepted (rollback). A hostile cache may withhold or reorder, never inject
cases.construction: cache of valid descriptors plus one whose _dmtap-mix attestation does not validate (variant: one absent from the log quorum); and separately, replay a cache at an older-or-equal version

expect.note: the unverifiable descriptor MUST NOT enter path selection

cases.checks: freeze defense is FAIL-QUEUED, not fail-closed: a derived view (or cache) older than the mix-directory freshness window (§16.3, <= one mix-key epoch) is stale — the client MUST refresh before building any private path and, if it cannot obtain a fresh one, MUST queue and retry. It MUST NOT downgrade the tier and MUST NOT refuse to enqueue: a directory outage delays mail, it must never stop it
cases.construction: freeze the client's log/cache feed at a view older than the freshness window; submit a private-tier MOTE from the user and inspect both the path build and the queue

expect.action: FAIL-QUEUED (§10.7.0): path build fails, enqueue succeeds — the MOTE is durably held and retried
expect.note: emitting a tier downgrade (0x0310 as a demotion) or refusing the enqueue is non-conformant

cases.checks: guards are drawn from a persistent sampled set, never re-sampled from the fleet on rotation: the active G guards MUST come only from the node's guard sample, chosen once; rotation moves the active guards inside the sample. The sample is refreshed only on exhaustion (sampled nodes permanently offline) or an explicit owner re-sample (a disclosed exposure event). Independent redraws turn the (1-f)^G bound into (1-f)^(G*r) — at G=2, 30-day rotation, f=0.05 a decade is 0.95^244 ~= 3e-7, i.e. near-certain eventual exposure (Tor proposal 271)
cases.construction: fleet of N attested, ASN-disjoint mixes held unchanged; record the node's initial guard sample; advance r >= 10 guard-rotation periods (§16.3) and record the active guards at each

expect.note: every active guard at every rotation is a member of the initial sample and the sample itself is unchanged; any guard outside it, absent exhaustion or an explicit re-sample, is non-conformant

cases.checks: ASN-disjointness in path selection: a path MUST traverse mixes under disjoint announced BGP origin ASNs. A candidate path whose hops carry three distinct attested operators but announce from one ASN MUST NOT be built — domains are cheap, attestation proves accountability rather than independence, and ASN is the axis rented capacity cannot cheaply diversify
cases.construction: three mixes with distinct, validly-attested operator domains, all reachable via addresses announced by a single BGP origin ASN; request a Standard-profile path

expect.note: the sender holds per §4.4.9 (0x0310 past the retry deadline) and MUST NOT route over the ASN-colliding path

cases.checks: an un-attested operator claim confers no diversity: a mix whose operator is absent or not backed by a valid _dmtap-mix attestation under that domain MUST NOT count as its own operator for the disjoint-operator rule — it is excluded from selection or counted as one unknown/shared operator. Otherwise a single adversary publishes N self-claimed operators and collapses the ~a^2 compromised-path bound to ~a
cases.construction: three mixes each self-asserting a different operator value, none carrying a resolvable/valid _dmtap-mix record; request a Standard-profile path

expect.note: counting the three self-claimed operators as three distinct operators is non-conformant

cases.checks: peer_id is per-epoch unlinkable: a node MUST derive peer_id freshly per location epoch rather than holding one identifier across its lifetime, so a peer_id observed at T cannot be linked to the same node at T+delta by the identifier alone. This is what bounds the harvest-now-decrypt-later exposure of the routing layer — a route recovered later resolves to an identifier that has expired
cases.construction: publish the node's LocationRecord for two consecutive location epochs e and e+1 under the same ik, both validly signed with strictly increasing seq; compare the peer_id fields

expect.note: peer_id(e) != peer_id(e+1) and both records verify; a stable lifetime peer_id is non-conformant

cases.checks: piggybacked location precedes every lookup: a resolver MUST try, in order, (1) the sender's signed LocationRecord carried by the MOTE itself, (2) cached direct addresses, (3) the home rendezvous set (>= 3 disjoint operators), (4) the DHT — opportunistic only, and a DHT-only record is a hint usable to attempt a connection but never to authenticate (identity is authenticated by the pinned IK, §3.4). Replying to a MOTE that carried a fresh valid record MUST perform no rendezvous query and no DHT lookup
cases.construction: instrumented resolver counting rendezvous and DHT queries; deliver a MOTE carrying the sender's current signed LocationRecord, then send a reply and read the counters

expect.note: zero lookups of any kind; the piggybacked record is used. Any lookup on this path is non-conformant — eclipse is an attack on lookup, and an established relationship must never depend on one

cases.checks: the zero-relationship floor delivers: a recipient MUST accept at least N_floor (§16.5, >= 5) cold MOTEs per sender-key per day from a sender presenting only a valid work proof — a sequential-work vdf (§9.4.1) or a memory-hard PoW (§9.4) — bound to the recipient's current epoch beacon, with no token, no postage and no vouch. Floor deliveries land in the requests area (§2.7a), never the inbox, and are not acked (DMTAP-VAL-12)
cases.construction: N_floor cold MOTEs from one fresh sender key inside 24 h, each carrying a valid work proof scoped per §9.2a (including sender_key) and no other credential; recipient has no relationship with the sender

expect.note: all N_floor MOTEs durably held in the requests area for the §16.5 retention; dropping any of them is non-conformant

cases.checks: a zero floor is a non-conformant policy: a recipient MAY grant far more than the floor to trusted issuers, vouched senders or paid postage, but MUST NOT grant less. N_floor = 0 (or below the §16.5 minimum) as a standing policy — including a shipped default, and including a reject-all-unknown-senders configuration offered without the §9.7a disclosure — MUST be refused rather than silently clamped. The §9.4 deferral budget under active flood is a transient budget, not a standing floor of zero
cases.construction: apply a recipient Policy (§9.2) with N_floor = 0; variant: a policy UI offering "reject all unknown senders" as a reachable configuration with no disclosure

expect.note: the node MUST NOT apply the sub-floor policy while reporting conformance

cases.checks: memory-hard PoW is the interoperable MUST floor: a conformant recipient MUST accept a valid memory-hard PoW solution (Argon2id at or above its advertised difficulty, scope including sender_key per §9.2a) as satisfying its cold-contact requirement, subject only to the §9.4 verification budget and its own rate policy. VDF is an OPTIONAL cost (MAY, §9.4.1) — it bounds aggregate parallelism but leaves a 10-100x latency advantage, its sequentiality is conjectural and processor-bounded, and it is not post-quantum, so it is neither the floor nor a SHOULD
cases.construction: cold MOTE carrying a valid Argon2id solution at the recipient's advertised difficulty and no VDF proof, from a sender with no relationship

expect.note: routed to the requests area exactly as DMTAP-FLOOR-01; rejecting it because it is not a VDF is non-conformant

cases.checks: VDF-only is a non-conformant policy: a recipient MUST NOT require a VDF as the only acceptable proof. A recipient that would accept only a VDF has made an unstandardized, non-post-quantum construction the price of contacting it, and a sender who cannot produce the one proof a recipient will take is simply undeliverable — the floor failing in a new way rather than holding
cases.construction: apply a ChallengeSpec/policy accepting vdf only, such that the valid memory-hard PoW of DMTAP-FLOOR-03 would be refused

cases.checks: no liveness failure may prevent enqueue: whatever is unreachable — KT logs, the derived fleet view, a postage issuer, the rendezvous set, every peer — a node MUST always be able to accept a message from its own user, hold it durably, and keep retrying under the guarantees in force. An offline-first store-and-forward protocol must never be unable to queue; email degrades to late, never to blocked
cases.construction: partition the node from every external dependency simultaneously; submit a private-tier message over the node's native client surface (§8.1); restart the node and re-inspect the queue

expect.note: message enqueued, still present and still retrying after a restart; refusing the submission for any unreachable dependency is non-conformant

cases.checks: each condition takes exactly its class's disposition: a FAIL-CLOSED-AUTH condition (a signature that does not verify, a pin mismatch, a log equivocation, a suite below the ratchet) is refused permanently — retrying cannot help and MUST NOT be offered or silently performed; a FAIL-QUEUED condition (an external service unreachable right now) MUST NOT be surfaced as a permanent rejection before the retry deadline (§16) and MUST NOT weaken any guarantee meanwhile. Classifying a liveness failure as fail-closed hands a denial-of-service surface to anyone who can take a service offline
cases.construction: drive one instance of each class against the same node — a tampered Payload.sig (§2.7 step 8, 0x0208) and a stale derived fleet view (§4.4.2, 0x0311) — and inspect the retry queue and the user-facing outcome for both

expect.note: the 0x0208 condition is permanent, never queued and never offered as retryable; the 0x0311 condition is queued and retried, never presented as a rejection before the retry deadline. Either mapping inverted is non-conformant

cases.checks: authorize, never classify (differential test): the gateway's admission decision MUST be a function of its permitted inputs only — SPF/DKIM/DMARC results, sending-IP standing, authenticated sender identity, cold-sender state, rate/volume counters. It MUST NOT run content-based scoring, Bayesian or learned filters, keyword/URL reputation or attachment-content heuristics, and MUST NOT drop, quarantine, re-rank or annotate a message on such a basis
cases.construction: two inbound legacy messages from the same authenticated sender inside its rate budget, identical in every authorization input (same envelope sender, same SPF/DKIM/DMARC result, same source IP, same cold-sender state), differing only in body/subject content — one carrying canonical spam-corpus text, one benign

expect.note: byte-identical admission decision, disposition and annotation for both messages; any divergence between them is proof of content classification and is non-conformant

cases.checks: no filter posture, no decryption for anti-abuse: a gateway MUST NOT be required to decrypt for anti-abuse purposes and MUST NOT present its authorization checks to users as a spam filter. Classification, where it happens at all, is recipient-side and on-device against the user's own corpus, and a recipient MUST be able to run no classifier and still be protected — the §9.1-§9.7a mechanisms are sender-cost and policy mechanisms, not content judgements
cases.construction: review of the gateway's user-facing copy, admin surfaces and operator documentation; no wire bytes to recompute

expect.outcome: non-conformant if the authorization gate is described to users as spam filtering, or if any anti-abuse path requires access to plaintext the gateway would not otherwise hold

cases.checks: privilege separation: gateway mode MUST run as a separate process in a separate privilege domain (distinct OS user; a distinct container/jail/namespace where the platform provides one) from the node's identity and store processes; it MUST NOT have access to identity key material (IK, device private keys, recovery material) and MUST NOT have read access to the local MOTE store. Legacy parsers (SMTP/IMAP/POP3/MIME/iCalendar/vCard) SHOULD be sandboxed further. One binary is a distribution property, never an isolation property, and the rule holds for a self-operated gateway too because the threat is remote code execution by a stranger, not the operator
cases.construction: deployment review: process table and uid separation, keystore and MOTE-store access permissions, sandbox profile; no wire bytes to recompute

expect.outcome: non-conformant if gateway mode shares an address space or privilege domain with the identity/store processes, or can read either

cases.checks: PubManifest.id over a single plaintext chunk: h_0=0x1e||BLAKE3-256(plaintext_0), id=0x1e||leaf(h_0), leaf(h)=BLAKE3-256(DS||0x00||h), DS='DMTAP-PUB-v0/manifest'||0x00
PubManifest.id over 3 ordered plaintext chunks (RFC 6962 split, k=2): MTH(h0,h1,h2) = node(node(leaf(h0),leaf(h1)),leaf(h2))
PubAnnounce.sig signing preimage: Ed25519(signer_seed).sign(DS||det_cbor(PubAnnounce minus key 9)), DS='DMTAP-PUB-v0/announce'||0x00
announce_id = 0x1e||BLAKE3-256(det_cbor(PubAnnounce)) over the complete, signed object (derived-anchor rule; no self-id field)

FeedEntry.prev-chain: genesis (seq=0, no prev) -> entry (seq=1, prev=genesis id) -> entry (seq=2, prev=prior id); FeedEntry_id is content-addressed by the generic §2.2 rule (see the vector's note: §22.4.1 does not spell this formula out the way §22.3.1 does for announce_id)

expect.note: and accept (prev_chain_valid)

cases.checks: FeedHead.sig signing preimage over v,suite,pub,seq,tip,ts,signer: Ed25519(signer_seed).sign(DS||det_cbor(FeedHead minus key 8)), DS='DMTAP-PUB-v0/feed'||0x00; tip transitively commits the whole log via the prev-chain

Manifest DS-tag confusion (sealed <-> public): the SAME ordered chunk-hash list, rooted once under the public DS-tagged tree and once under the §18.9.5 bare sealed tree (no DS fold), MUST yield different roots

expect.note: public_root_hex != sealed_style_root_hex

cases.checks: a PubManifest CBOR map carrying the forbidden key 5 MUST be rejected — a public blob has no key by construction
a recomputed DS-tagged Merkle root that does not equal PubManifest.id MUST be rejected before fetch begins
cases.construction: flip one byte of a chunk hash in pub_manifest_three_chunks and recompute the root

cases.checks: a fetched plaintext chunk whose recomputed h_i disagrees with its listed PubManifest.chunks entry MUST be rejected and refetched from another holder
cases.construction: tamper one byte of a chunk from pub_manifest_three_chunks and recompute h_i

cases.checks: a recomputed announce_id that does not equal the address the object was fetched by MUST be rejected
cases.construction: mutate one byte of the pub_announce_id vector's bytes_hex and recompute announce_id

expect.note: retryable (re-fetch)

cases.checks: sig failing under signer, or signer not authorized by pub (no valid DeviceCert chain), MUST be rejected
cases.construction: flip one bit of pub_announce_signing_preimage's sig_hex and re-verify against pubkey_hex

cases.checks: a supersedes reference to an announce whose pub differs from this announce's pub MUST be rejected; a publisher may only supersede its own announcements

a FeedHead.seq strictly below the highest accepted for that pub MUST be rejected; equal seq + identical tip is an idempotent accept (cacheable re-fetch, never an error); equal seq + different tip is equivocation (-> PUB-15), never a rollback
two FeedEntrys at one seq with the same prev (or a prev not resolving to seq-1) is evidence of a rewritten/equivocated author log
a genesis entry (seq=0) carrying a prev field, or a non-genesis entry (seq!=0) lacking one, is malformed
FeedHead.sig failing under the signer/pub chain MUST be rejected
cases.construction: flip one bit of pub_feed_head_signing_preimage's sig_hex and re-verify

cases.checks: a PubAnnounce/PubManifest/FeedHead carrying a v/suite the implementation does not support MUST be rejected, never guessed
cases.construction: PubAnnounce with v=1 (any value != 0)

cases.checks: a holder declining to serve a requested public object per its own serve policy is a policy deny at the holder, never a correctness error; the fetcher rotates to another holder
cases.construction: holder policy configured to decline a given announce_id/manifest_root

expect.note: fetcher ROTATE_RETRY

cases.checks: exceeding a serving node's admission policy (object size / per-publisher quota / feed-append rate) is a policy deny, never a security/crypto gate
cases.construction: publish/append past a configured per-publisher storage quota

cases.checks: a client MUST NOT create/announce/serve a public object except as the direct result of an explicit user publish act; MUST warn, before completing a publish, that publishing is irrevocable; MUST express retraction only as a successor supersedes announcement, never as deletion
cases.construction: implementer/UX review against the three §22.7 sub-requirements

expect.outcome: non-conformant if any of the three sub-requirements is missing

cases.checks: every artifact pub_announce's ArtifactMetadata carries a license field (SPDX expression); an announce omitting it is malformed for this profile
cases.construction: ArtifactMetadata with key 7 (license) omitted

expect.note: profile-level; a generic §22 node still stores/serves it unaffected

cases.checks: formats contains at least one entry
cases.construction: ArtifactMetadata with formats (key 4) an empty array

cases.checks: exactly one formats entry carries role=canonical-source (non-assembly kinds) or role=structure (assembly kind); an assembly with no structure entry is malformed
cases.construction: two role=1 entries (ambiguous canonical source); and separately, an assembly-kind with no role=3 entry

cases.checks: no format_id=3 (glTF/mesh) entry ever carries role=1 (canonical-source) — a lossy tessellation is never the artifact of record
cases.construction: ArtifactFormat{format_id:3, role:1, manifest_root:<any hash>}

cases.checks: every role=2 (derived-rendition) entry carries derived_from_format (key 4)
cases.construction: ArtifactFormat{format_id:1, role:2} with derived_from_format omitted

cases.checks: units.length_unit is present and explicit; a client MUST NOT default or infer it
cases.construction: Units with key 1 (length_unit) omitted

expect.note: client MUST refuse to interpret geometry; MAY still show name/description/license

cases.checks: deprecated=true is always accompanied by deprecation_reason (key 9)
cases.construction: ArtifactMetadata{deprecated: true} with key 9 absent

expect.note: flagged by a CAD-aware index

cases.checks: deprecation/yank is expressed ONLY as a successor announcement (supersedes + deprecated=true), never as a deletion — no operation removes a previously published revision
cases.construction: attempt to model 'deletion' of a prior revision (no protocol operation exists; a CAD-aware client MUST NOT present one)

expect.note: client MUST NOT imply deletion

cases.checks: assembly children reference exclusively by pin (ref_kind=1, a manifest_root) or track (ref_kind=2, a pub_announce id)
cases.construction: AssemblyChild.ref_kind outside {1,2}

cases.checks: a BOM-walking client MUST detect and reject a cycle in an assembly's resolved DAG (a track reference can form one across revisions) rather than recurse indefinitely or silently drop it
cases.construction: assembly A tracks part B; B's publisher later republishes B as an assembly that tracks back to A; walk A's BOM

expect.note: abort the walk at the cycle and surface it — never infinite-recurse, never silently drop

cases.checks: no client treats any single index (category/search/workshop) as authoritative over the signed announces/feeds it was derived from
cases.construction: two independently-built indexes over the same feed set disagree (different crawl coverage)

expect.note: neither index is 'wrong'; ground truth is always the signed announces, re-derivable by any client

cases.checks: every VideoManifest carries a license field (SPDX expression or a profile consent token all-rights-reserved/mirror-freely/endorsed-only)
cases.construction: VideoManifest with key 9 (license) omitted

expect.note: profile-level; a generic §22 node still stores/serves it unaffected

cases.checks: original (key 5) is present and is the canonical rendition — a Rendition is never the artifact of record
cases.construction: VideoManifest with original (key 5) omitted; and separately, a client treating a Rendition.blob as the source of truth

cases.checks: every Rendition carries produced_by (key 8) and a derivation_sig (key 9) that verifies over the derivation statement
cases.construction: Rendition with derivation_sig omitted; and separately, a derivation_sig that fails to verify over the reconstructed statement

expect.note: not an authorized rendition; MAY be shown labeled as unverified, or dropped

cases.checks: a rendition is treated as authorized only if produced_by is the manifest author or holds an unrevoked, unexpired rendition delegate grant from the author
cases.construction: a validly-signed Rendition whose produced_by is neither the author nor a valid delegate

expect.note: present only as a labeled third-party encoding, never as an equivalent authorized rendition

cases.checks: the derivation statement binds derived_from->rendition.blob + codec/width/height/bitrate, signed under "DMTAP-VID-v0/derivation" by a device key chaining to produced_by
cases.construction: signable KAT recipe: stmt = det_cbor([derived_from, rendition.blob, codec, width, height, bitrate]); sig = Sign(dev_key, "DMTAP-VID-v0/derivation" || 0x00 || BLAKE3-256(stmt)); mutate any tuple element => signature MUST fail

cases.checks: a video's channel (key 10) reference resolves to an announce with the same pub as the video's author (a video cannot join another identity's channel)
cases.construction: VideoManifest.channel naming a Channel announce with a different pub

cases.checks: retracted = true (key 12) is always accompanied by retract_reason (key 13)
cases.construction: VideoManifest{retracted: true} with key 13 absent

expect.note: malformed for this profile

cases.checks: retraction/removal is expressed only as a successor supersedes announcement (retracted=true), never as deletion; a client MUST NOT imply deletion of prior bytes
cases.construction: attempt to model 'deletion' of a prior revision (no protocol operation exists)

expect.note: no-such-operation; retracted bytes remain fetchable, only status changes

cases.checks: a threaded Comment with a parent (key 3) references the same subject (key 1) as its parent
cases.construction: Comment whose parent references a comment with a different subject

expect.note: parent-subject mismatch

cases.checks: a Reaction is counted at most once per identity per subject — a later same-author reaction supersedes the earlier
cases.construction: two reactions by one identity to one subject, the later supersedes the earlier; an index counts both

expect.note: count the current one only; superseded reactions are discarded

cases.checks: segmented playback verifies segment/range bytes against the signed rendition's Merkle root; the HLS/DASH playlist is unsigned serving output, never authoritative
cases.construction: a PUB-server-supplied .m3u8 listing a segment whose bytes do not verify against the rendition root; client accepts on the playlist's say-so

expect.note: verify against the signed root; the playlist is not an object of record

cases.checks: live streaming is gated behind the vid-live-1 capability; a consumer lacking it treats its absence as a fact, not a fault (capability-absence rule §21.22)
cases.construction: a peer that has not advertised vid-live-1 receives a LiveManifest; treats non-support as a parse failure

expect.note: absence is unsupported-not-fatal; store the announce, do not low-latency-follow

cases.checks: view/reaction/trending aggregates are presented as per-server claims, never as network-wide truth
cases.construction: a client renders a PUB server's view count as an authoritative global number

expect.note: label as 'views on this server'; a signed tally is worth only the attester's reputation

cases.checks: no client treats any single index (search/recommendation/compliance) as authoritative over the signed announces/feeds it was derived from
cases.construction: two independently-built indexes (or compliance feeds) over the same feed set disagree

expect.note: neither is 'wrong'; ground truth is the signed feeds, re-derivable by any client

cases.checks: encrypted-media fields (keygrant/encryption) do not appear in a public VideoManifest — the public profile makes no confidentiality claim
cases.construction: a VideoManifest carrying an encryption/keygrant field

expect.note: category error; encrypted media belongs to the sealed path §5.5, not this public profile

pub_vectors_count_note: pub_vectors.json is a SEPARATE file from vectors.json, generated by vectors/gen_pub_vectors.py (this repo), not by the dmtap-core reference crate (which does not yet implement the DMTAP-PUB extension, §22, ROADMAP.md). All 15 of its vectors are referenced by PUB cases below (several PUB cases reference more than one pub_vectors.json entry).
